steps.text: Open the Todo widget from the header to create your first todo.

showcase.title: Installed apps are always available
showcase.description: Plugin commands can expose native widgets directly in the web UI.

steps.text: Click New to open the widget-native add form.

showcase.title: Commands can become interfaces
showcase.description: The same command surface can render forms, actions, and rich outputs.

steps.text: Click to fill the todo title.

showcase.title: Type once, store it as app data
showcase.description: The todo is created as structured plugin state, ready for follow-up actions.

steps.text: Click Add to create the todo.
Hover the todo row to reveal its row actions.

showcase.title: Structured data stays interactive
showcase.description: Outputs are not screenshots. They remain live app surfaces with follow-up actions.

steps.text: Click the row actions menu.
Click Add sibling to open another inline add form.
Click to fill the sibling todo title.

showcase.title: Keep related work grouped together
showcase.description: Sibling items make it easy to grow a small checklist from one starting point.

steps.text: Click Add to create the sibling todo.
Hover the parent todo row again to add a child item.

showcase.title: Nested workflows are first-class
showcase.description: Todos can become trees, so plans can carry subtasks where they belong.

steps.text: Click the row actions menu again.
Click Add child to open an inline child form.
Click to fill the child todo title.

showcase.title: Capture the next concrete step
showcase.description: Child todos keep implementation details attached to the larger task.

arguments.text: Share demo notes with the team

steps.text: Click Add to create the child todo.

steps.text: Open the Todo widget from the header to use its AI prompt.

showcase.title: AI lives next to the app
showcase.description: Plugins can provide focused AI flows that know their own data model.

steps.text: Click Fill to fill the AI prompt for me.

showcase.title: Write here and let AI draft items for you
showcase.description: Describe the outcome in natural language while the plugin handles the app-specific shape.

arguments.prompt: Create follow-up tasks for a live AppWeaver demo: prepare notes, schedule a dry run, and send the recap.

steps.text: Click Run AI to generate the todos.

showcase.title: Agents can prepare app-native changes
showcase.description: The model proposes changes through the plugin instead of directly mutating state.

steps.text: Click Accept on the draft review to create the todos.

showcase.title: Draft items can be accepted or revised
showcase.description: Review the AI proposal, accept it when it is right, or revise before anything is written.

story.title: Prioritize todos with duel
story.description: Use the Todo duel widget to rank sibling tasks through interactive pairwise choices.

showcase.title: Interactive widgets can guide app workflows
showcase.description: Duel asks simple A/B questions, records comparisons, and turns a flat task list into a ranked priority order.

items.description: Use duel to rank the next demo polish tasks.
Ensure the landing page explains plugin installation clearly.
Make the first screen feel intentional on small devices.

items.description: Use duel to rank the next demo polish tasks.
Ensure the landing page explains plugin installation clearly.
Make the first screen feel intentional on small devices.
Check play, pause, rewind, and next controls in the app demo.

steps.text: Open the Todo widget from the header to rank sibling tasks.

showcase.title: Todo can ask focused interactive questions
showcase.description: The duel command compares sibling tasks one pair at a time instead of asking you to sort the whole list manually.

steps.text: Hover the parent row to reveal its row actions.
Open the row action menu.
Click Duel to start pairwise ranking for the child tasks.

showcase.title: Commands can update in place for structured input
showcase.description: The widget replaces itself after each A/B choice so the ranking flow stays in one card.

steps.text: Choose A for the first comparison.

showcase.title: Each answer records one comparison
showcase.description: Simple A/B choices are enough for the plugin to build a ranking graph.

steps.text: Choose A again for the second comparison.
Choose A for the final comparison.
Click Done to return to the ranked todo list.

showcase.title: Duel returns to the regular list
showcase.description: When the ranking is complete, Done closes the duel view and shows the updated order.

story.title: Schedule a recurring agent job
story.description: Use the Jobs widget AI prompt to draft and confirm a recurring agent workflow.

showcase.title: Agents can work on a schedule
showcase.description: Turn a natural-language request into a reviewed, recurring job that will run later without leaving the app.

widgetHelp.title: Job scheduler
body: Schedule recurring or one-time tasks by giving natural prompts like "Remind me of a meeting on Monday morning."

stories.title: Schedule a recurring agent job
stories.description: Use the Jobs widget AI prompt to draft and confirm a recurring agent workflow.

props.inputPlaceholder: Add jobs using a prompt like "Remind me to check email tomorrow at 9am"

children.value: No jobs. Use /job ai <prompt> to add one.

widgetHelp.title: Job scheduler
body: Schedule recurring or one-time tasks by giving natural prompts like "Remind me of a meeting on Monday morning."

stories.title: Schedule a recurring agent job
stories.description: Use the Jobs widget AI prompt to draft and confirm a recurring agent workflow.

props.inputPlaceholder: Add jobs using a prompt like "Remind me to check email tomorrow at 9am"

children.value: Prompt: Review open todos, identify the three highest priority items, and send a concise morning brief.

AI drafted this scheduled job:

type         : cron
schedule     : 0 9 * * 1-5
maxRuns      : -
name         : Morning priority brief
prompt       : Review open todos, identify the three highest priority items, and send a concise morning brief.
description  : Every weekday at 09:00
backend      : opencode
provider     : local
model        : default
mode         : agent
budget_sats  : -
instructions : Keep the brief actionable and include only items that can be started today.

Draft ID: demo-job-draft-1
a=accept, r=revise, d=decline, s=skip, q=quit

steps.text: Open the Jobs widget from the header to create a scheduled workflow.

showcase.title: Scheduled work starts as a focused app flow
showcase.description: The Jobs plugin exposes a widget for creating, reviewing, and managing recurring agent tasks.

steps.text: Click Fill to describe the job in natural language.

showcase.title: Describe the schedule and the work together
showcase.description: The plugin turns timing, recurrence, and runtime instructions into a structured job draft.

arguments.prompt: Every weekday morning at 9, review my open todos and send me the top three priorities for the day.

steps.text: Click Run AI to generate the job draft.

showcase.title: AI drafts, it does not silently mutate
showcase.description: The generated job is reviewed before it is added to the scheduler.

steps.text: Click Accept on the draft review to schedule the job.

showcase.title: Review scheduled automation before it goes live
showcase.description: Confirm the cron schedule, prompt, execution context, and budget before the job is stored.

story.title: Save a bookmark with AI

showcase.description: Paste a URL, let the plugin draft metadata, then accept the bookmark only after review.

steps.text: Open the Bookmarks widget from the header to save a resource.

showcase.title: Installed apps can capture useful context
showcase.description: Bookmarks are stored as structured records with category, tags, media type, queue state, and summaries.

steps.text: Click Fill to enter the URL and save instructions.

showcase.title: One prompt becomes complete bookmark metadata
showcase.description: The bookmark plugin fills the title, summary, category, tags, media type, and queue state together.

arguments.prompt: Save https://nostr.com/ as a queued reading bookmark, summarize it, and tag it for nostr and appweaver.

steps.text: Click Run AI to draft the bookmark.

showcase.title: Draft first, write later
showcase.description: AI-created bookmarks are proposed for review before they change the collection.

steps.text: Click Accept on the draft review to save the bookmark.

showcase.title: Humans stay in control of the knowledge base
showcase.description: Accept, revise, or decline the draft before it becomes app data.

widgetHelp.title: Bookmark manager
body: Keep your bookmarks tidy with the help of AI. Access them from anywhere, search public bookmarks ordered by your network, and publish yours when you want.

stories.title: Save a bookmark with AI
stories.description: Use the Bookmarks widget AI prompt to save, summarize, categorize, and queue a resource.

story.title: Read markdown from the file tree
story.description: Use the File widget tree to open a markdown document in the built-in reader.

showcase.title: The workspace tree is an app surface
showcase.description: Browse project files, expand folders, and open markdown or code without leaving the chat-native UI.

widgetHelp.title: File manager
body: Browse project files, open markdown or code, inspect history, and jump into diffs without leaving the app.

stories.title: Read markdown from the file tree
stories.description: Use the File widget tree to open a markdown document in the built-in reader.

stories.title: Open a git diff from the tree
stories.description: Use git status in the file tree to open a workspace diff view.

props.ttsText:
# Demo System

Stories are small guided walkthroughs that can run inside the app and in the landing page demo.

They combine scripted command outputs with live web UI actions, so a plugin can teach its own workflow without depending on real workspace state.

This file view is rendered by the File plugin markdown/code reader.

children.value: Stories are small guided walkthroughs that can run inside the app and in the landing page demo.
They combine scripted command outputs with live web UI actions, so a plugin can teach its own workflow without depending on real workspace state.
This file view is rendered by the File plugin markdown/code reader.
+- Preview plugin stories in the web demo
+- Browse files with markdown and diff views

steps.text: Open the Files widget from the header to browse the workspace tree.

showcase.title: Files are navigable inside the app
showcase.description: The tree widget can be used as a compact workspace browser in demos and real sessions.

steps.text: Click DEMO_SYSTEM.md to open it in the markdown reader.

showcase.title: Tree rows can launch follow-up commands
showcase.description: A file row opens the File plugin reader with syntax highlighting for markdown and code.

steps.text: Open the Files widget from the header to inspect git status.

showcase.title: Workspace state is visible at a glance
showcase.description: The same tree can show clean files, modified files, and diff actions.

steps.text: Click the modified badge beside README.md to open its diff.

story.title: Capture and publish a journal entry
story.description: Write a private diary entry from the Journal widget and find the publish action in the entry menu.

showcase.title: Private first, publish when ready
showcase.description: Captain's Log keeps notes local by default and only publishes a selected entry when you choose it.

widgetHelp.title: Captain's Log
body: A private page for what happened, what mattered, and what should not be forgotten. Publish any of them as a short note to your Nostr followers.

stories.title: Capture and publish a journal entry
stories.description: Write a private log entry from the widget, then use the entry menu when you want to publish it to Nostr.

props.inputPlaceholder: What do you want to remember? Tags like #idea or #stuck are picked up automatically.

children.value: Shipped a smaller diary page surface and kept the entry actions out of the way until hover. #journal

steps.text: Open Captain's Log from the widget dock.

showcase.title: The diary is a persistent widget
showcase.description: The journal opens as a singleton timeline widget so it stays available while you work.

steps.text: Write a short entry in Quick capture.

showcase.title: Capture one sentence
showcase.description: The widget is optimized for quick private notes rather than heavy document editing.

arguments.text: Captured the key decision before it disappeared. #demo

steps.text: Use the entry menu later when you want to publish a private note to Nostr.

showcase.title: Publishing is explicit
showcase.description: The entry menu keeps Edit, Delete, and Publish actions close to the note without crowding the page.
